source: verify-direction negative corpus pinning the SS3.15 STRICT RFC 8032 consensus verifier (include/determ/crypto/ed25519/ed25519.h determ_ed25519_verify): PASS vectors are RFC 8032 SS7.1 TEST 1/2/3 published values transcribed exactly (anchor-grade); FAIL vectors are mechanically constructed on 2026-07-03 (gen_ed25519_verify_strict.py, generating session scratchpad) from the PASS material: S'=S+L little-endian malleation per PASS vector (the (R,S+L) twin a lenient verifier may accept - strict S < L gate MUST reject), 1-bit flips in R and in msg, and non-canonical pubkey encodings y=q+1 >= q with both sign-bit variants (q = 2^255-19, L = 2^252 + 27742317777372353535851937790883648493)

_meta.oracle_note: all 10 vectors confirmed against libsodium: every PASS verifies, every FAIL raises (libsodium enforces S < L and canonical pk)
_meta.results: PASS = determ_ed25519_verify must return 0; FAIL = determ_ed25519_verify must return -1 (reject)

vectors.name: RFC 8032 7.1 TEST 1 (empty message) malleated S'=S+L - strict verifier MUST reject (S >= L)
RFC 8032 7.1 TEST 2 (1-byte message 0x72) malleated S'=S+L - strict verifier MUST reject (S >= L)
RFC 8032 7.1 TEST 3 (2-byte message 0xaf82) malleated S'=S+L - strict verifier MUST reject (S >= L)
RFC 8032 7.1 TEST 1 (empty message) with R bit 0 of byte 0 flipped - MUST reject
RFC 8032 7.1 TEST 2 (1-byte message 0x72) with msg bit 0 of byte 0 flipped (0x72 -> 0x73) - MUST reject
non-canonical pubkey y=q+1 sign_bit=0 (y >= q) - MUST reject at pk decode
non-canonical pubkey y=q+1 sign_bit=1 (y >= q) - MUST reject at pk decode
